story.iWant: to see a 6-slide sample deck demonstrating my theme
story.soThat: I can visually validate the extracted brand before building real slides

subtasks.subtask: 1.1: Create logic to read theme.json from `.slide-builder/theme.json`
1.2: Define 6 sample slide specifications matching tech spec table
1.3: Create sample content appropriate for each slide type
2.1: Invoke frontend-design skill for each of 6 slides with theme context
2.2: Pass full theme.json to skill for CSS variable usage
2.3: Pass slide type and sample content as context
2.4: Report progress after each slide generation
3.1: Ensure generated HTML includes viewport meta tag for 1920x1080
3.2: Ensure CSS :root block includes all theme CSS variables
3.3: Ensure slide content uses CSS variables (not hardcoded values)
3.4: Add data-slide-id attribute to slide container
4.1: Create `.slide-builder/samples/` directory if not exists
4.2: Save each slide to correct filename: `sample-{n}-{type}.html`
4.3: Verify all 6 files written successfully
4.4: Output file paths in completion message
5.1: Expand Phase 4 placeholder in instructions.md
5.2: Add action to load theme.json
5.3: Add loop to invoke frontend-design skill 6 times
5.4: Add action to save each slide to samples/ directory
5.5: Add progress output after each slide
5.6: Add completion output with all file paths
5.7: Update status.yaml with phase: "sample-generation-complete"
6.1: Test with complete theme.json from Phase 3
6.2: Verify all 6 files created in samples/ directory
6.3: Open each slide in browser, verify renders correctly
6.4: Check 1920x1080 dimensions via DevTools
6.5: Inspect CSS variables in DevTools, verify theme values used
6.6: Verify each slide tests its designated primitives

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Given a theme.json has been generated, when sample deck generation runs, then 6 HTML slides are created in `.slide-builder/samples/`

Sample slides include: title (hero typography, primary color), list (body text, bullets), flow (arrows, boxes), columns (multiple box styles), callout (accent color, emphasis), code (mono font, alt background)
Each slide uses theme primitives via CSS variables
Slides can be opened in browser for preview

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Invokes frontend-design skill 6 times with theme context. Each slide targets specific primitives per PRD sample deck table.

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.snippet: Slides are self-contained HTML files using CSS variables from theme.json. Output to .slide-builder/samples/ directory.

doc.title: Epics and Stories
doc.snippet: Generate 6-slide sample deck demonstrating all theme primitives for visual validation.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: 6 slides testing: title/hero typography, list/bullets, flow/arrows, columns/comparison, callout/accent, code/mono font.

doc.title: Previous Story: Theme File Generation
doc.snippet: theme.json structure complete with meta, colors, typography, shapes, layouts placeholder, personality. CSS variable mapping established.

file.reason: Main file to modify - Phase 4 placeholder needs expansion with sample generation steps
INPUT: Theme data to read for CSS variable generation. Contains colors, typography, shapes.
Status file to update with phase: "sample-generation-complete"

EXISTING REFERENCE: Example implementation showing HTML structure, CSS variables pattern, 1920x1080 viewport. NOTE: Current naming uses 01-title.html, story spec uses sample-1-title.html - verify naming convention.
Reference for coding patterns and file naming conventions

node.usage: Browser automation for potential screenshot/preview functionality
Google Slides export (Epic 7 - not used in this story)

constraint.rule: ADR-001: 100% alignment with BMAD workflow pattern. Steps must use <action>, <check>, <output> tags
ADR-002: Hybrid approach - samples become layout templates after user approval (Story 2.5)
Slides must be self-contained HTML files with no external dependencies except Google Fonts
HTML must include viewport meta tag for 1920x1080 and CSS :root block with all theme variables
Must invoke frontend-design skill via Skill tool call for each slide generation. Skill provides design expertise.
NOTE: Existing samples use numeric prefix naming (01-title.html). Tech spec specifies sample-{n}-{type}.html. Verify and standardize naming convention.
NFR1: Sample deck generation should complete within reasonable time
NFR18: HTML slides must render correctly in Chrome, Firefox, Safari

interface.name: theme.json
CSS Variables
interface.path: CSS :root block in each HTML slide

interface.name: status.yaml
interface.signature: phase: "sample-generation-complete" samples: directory: .slide-builder/samples/ count: 6 files: [list of filenames]

tests.standards: Per tech spec Test Strategy: Manual browser testing with Frontend Test Gate pattern. Each slide must be opened in browser and visually verified. Use DevTools to check dimensions (1920x1080), CSS variables usage, and console for errors. Cross-browser testing in Chrome, Firefox, Safari.

ideas.idea: Verify exactly 6 HTML files exist in .slide-builder/samples/ after generation completes
Check each file is valid HTML that parses without errors
Visual inspection: Title slide shows hero typography and primary color
Visual inspection: List slide shows body text with proper bullet styling
Visual inspection: Flow slide shows arrows and boxes with connectors
Visual inspection: Columns slide shows multiple box styles aligned
Visual inspection: Callout slide shows accent color with emphasis styling
Visual inspection: Code slide shows mono font with alt background
DevTools: Inspect computed styles, verify CSS variables (--color-primary etc.) are used
DevTools: Verify no hardcoded color/font values in element styles
Open each .html file directly in browser via file:// protocol - should render without server
Check no console errors on page load
Cross-browser: Test in Chrome, Firefox, Safari for consistent rendering
